fig:free_energy_curve.alt: Line plot of free energy in nats versus coupling strength lambda for the entangled posterior relative to a mean-field prior. A single dark curve traces free energy across the sweep; the minimum is marked with a teal scatter point and labeled with the argmin lambda value.
fig:free_energy_curve.caption: Free energy of the entangled posterior relative to the mean-field prior across the hyperparameter sweep (grid points {{param_sweep_grid_points}}).
fig:free_energy_curve.label: fig:free_energy_curve

fig:gnn_ontology_concordance.alt: Layered concordance diagram linking analytical symbols, GNN variables from bernoulli_toy.gnn.md ({{gnn_spec_version}}), and Active Inference Ontology terms.
fig:gnn_ontology_concordance.caption: GNN $\leftrightarrow$ ontology concordance for the Bernoulli–Ising toy ({{gnn_spec_version}}).
fig:gnn_ontology_concordance.label: fig:gnn_ontology_concordance

fig:invariant_dashboard.alt: Horizontal bar checklist of analytical and simulation invariant names with pass or fail status. {{invariants_passed}} of {{invariants_total}} checks pass on the merged report.
fig:invariant_dashboard.caption: Invariant dashboard: {{invariants_passed}} / {{invariants_total}} merged analytical and simulation checks from the validation registry.
fig:invariant_dashboard.label: fig:invariant_dashboard

fig:ising_mi_curve.alt: Two-panel plot of mutual information versus coupling strength lambda for the symmetric Bernoulli-Ising toy. Left panel shows the closed-form curve (solid dark line) and an independent exact recomputation via total correlation (dashed blue line) with lambda on the x-axis and mutual information in nats on the y-axis. Right panel shows the recompute-minus-closed-form residual versus lambda with a zero reference line; the two deterministic estimators agree to machine precision.
fig:ising_mi_curve.caption: Closed-form $I(\lambda)$ and an independent exact recomputation via total correlation for the symmetric Bernoulli-Ising toy across {{param_sweep_grid_points}} grid points up to $\lambda_{\max}$ = {{lambda_max}}; grid maximum {{ising_mi_saturation}} nats. Both estimators are deterministic (no sampling), so the right panel is a cross-implementation agreement check (max residual {{sweep_max_residual}} nats), not a sampling residual.
fig:ising_mi_curve.label: fig:ising_mi_curve

fig:lean_boundary_status.alt: Table figure listing Lean modules, declaration kinds, names, and proved versus sorry status under lean/TemplateActiveInference/.
fig:lean_boundary_status.caption: Lean formalization boundary: module witnesses checked by lake build.
fig:lean_boundary_status.label: fig:lean_boundary_status

fig:multi_track_architecture.alt: Process diagram linking three scientific tracks to {{pipeline_track_count}} pipeline gates and {{sheaf_track_count}} sheaf fragment types across {{imrad_manifest_rows}} manifest rows.
fig:multi_track_architecture.caption: Multi-track architecture: analytical, pymdp, and sheaf composition lanes mapped to {{pipeline_track_count}} pipeline gates and {{sheaf_track_count}} composable fragment types.
fig:multi_track_architecture.label: fig:multi_track_architecture

fig:semantic_gluing_graph.alt: Dependency diagram linking configured analysis scripts to generated evidence artifacts, manuscript consumers, and validation gates for the semantic sheaf gluing certificate.
fig:semantic_gluing_graph.caption: Semantic gluing graph: configured producers, generated evidence artifacts, and validation consumers for the multi-track sheaf certificate.
fig:semantic_gluing_graph.label: fig:semantic_gluing_graph

fig:sheaf_coverage_heatmap.alt: Heatmap matrix of IMRAD manuscript rows versus {{sheaf_track_count}} sheaf fragment track columns. Black cells mean the track is bound and the fragment file exists; white cells mean the track is not bound; gray cells mean bound but missing. Rows are grouped by IMRAD block with indented subsection labels; column headers list track ids.
fig:sheaf_coverage_heatmap.caption: Sheaf track coverage matrix: {{imrad_manifest_rows}} IMRAD rows × {{sheaf_track_count}} fragment columns. Black = present (P), white = absent (—), gray = missing (M). Counts: {{coverage_present}} present / {{coverage_bound}} bound / {{coverage_missing}} missing.
fig:sheaf_coverage_heatmap.label: fig:sheaf_coverage_heatmap

fig:sheaf_layers_overview.alt: Two-panel overview of sheaf fragment layers. Left panel shows {{sheaf_track_count}} composable track types in registry compose order with labels and renderer ids. Right panel shows the IMRAD section binding heatmap with black present, white absent, and gray missing cells across {{imrad_manifest_rows}} manifest rows and {{sheaf_track_count}} tracks.
fig:sheaf_layers_overview.caption: Sheaf layers overview: registry stack (compose order, renderer ids) and IMRAD binding heatmap for {{sheaf_track_count}} fragment types across {{imrad_manifest_rows}} manifest rows ({{coverage_present}} present / {{coverage_bound}} bound / {{coverage_missing}} missing).
fig:sheaf_layers_overview.label: fig:sheaf_layers_overview

fig:si_belief_entropy_curve.alt: Line plot of belief entropy in nats versus timestep for the pymdp T-maze rollout. Entropy ranges from {{si_entropy_min}} to {{si_entropy_max}} nats across {{si_tmaze_steps}} steps in {{pymdp_mode}} mode.
fig:si_belief_entropy_curve.caption: Belief entropy over time for the T-maze rollout (mean {{si_tmaze_mean_belief_entropy_formatted}} nats).
fig:si_belief_entropy_curve.label: fig:si_belief_entropy_curve

fig:si_obs_action_trace.alt: Dual-panel plot of observation index and action index versus timestep for the pymdp T-maze rollout. The upper panel shows discrete observations; the lower panel shows actions. Goal reached flag is {{si_goal_reached}}.
fig:si_obs_action_trace.caption: Observation and action traces for the T-maze rollout (action diversity {{si_action_diversity}}).
fig:si_obs_action_trace.label: fig:si_obs_action_trace

fig:si_tmaze_actions.alt: Step plot of discrete action index versus timestep for the pymdp T-maze rollout in {{pymdp_mode}} mode. Actions change at each timestep with light fill under the step trace; policy depth is {{si_tmaze_policy_len}} steps.
fig:si_tmaze_actions.caption: Discrete action index over time for the pymdp T-maze rollout (policy length {{si_tmaze_policy_len}}).
fig:si_tmaze_actions.label: fig:si_tmaze_actions

fig:tmaze_schematic.alt: Schematic of the minimal T-maze POMDP with start and goal states, discrete actions and observations, and planning horizon policy_len = {{si_tmaze_policy_len}}.
fig:tmaze_schematic.caption: T-maze generative model schematic ({{si_tmaze_policy_len}}-step policy horizon, {{pymdp_mode}} mode).
fig:tmaze_schematic.label: fig:tmaze_schematic
